refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under controlled duty cycle conditions with continuous telemetry capture of cycle start/end timestamps, segmented by load, travel, and dump phases. Statistical analysis of p95 percentile against CT_target baseline.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions within motion envelope constraints to support cycle time target.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness (latency, bandwidth) sufficient to achieve cycle time target when tele-operated.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding a defined safety-overhead time budget that erodes cycle time.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration) to enable KPI tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-2a: Optimize vehicle powertrain, hydraulics, and control algorithms to minimize motion cycle duration while respecting safety and duty cycle constraints.
DP-2b: Implement low-latency teleoperation network and control stack to minimize operator-induced delays in tele-operated mode.
DP-2c: Design safety services to enforce safety constraints with minimal timing overhead via predictive safety checks and parallel execution.
DP-2d: Instrument the vehicle and teleoperation stack with cycle time measurement and telemetry reporting to enable real-time and post-hoc KPI analysis.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Decompose cycle time performance into four coupled sub-problems: (1) vehicle motion optimization (powertrain, hydraulics, control), (2) teleoperation latency minimization (network, control stack), (3) safety overhead budgeting (predictive checks, parallel execution), and (4) telemetry instrumentation (measurement, reporting). Manage coupling via shared duty cycle definition, safety time budget allocation, and integrated performance testing.

potential_sources: Vehicle motion optimization and safety services are tightly coupled: safety constraints (e.g., speed limits, acceleration limits) directly constrain vehicle motion envelope and cycle time.
Teleoperation latency and vehicle control are coupled: operator responsiveness depends on both network latency and vehicle control loop bandwidth.
Safety overhead and cycle time are coupled: safety checks (e.g., collision avoidance, stability monitoring) may introduce delays that erode cycle time.
Telemetry instrumentation and all sub-systems are coupled: measurement points must be distributed across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP exhibits moderate-to-high coupling across vehicle dynamics, teleoperation, safety, and instrumentation domains. This is inherent to the KPI requirement and cannot be eliminated; however, coupling must be managed via explicit interface contracts (duty cycle, safety time budget, telemetry schema) and integrated testing.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within the vehicle's physical motion envelope and safety constraints. Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy it; requires validation against vehicle dynamics simulation and safety analysis.

Assumption: Teleoperation latency can be reduced to acceptable levels via network optimization. Risk: Physical network constraints (e.g., satellite uplink, 4G coverage) may impose hard latency floors incompatible with CT_target.

Assumption: Safety overhead can be budgeted and allocated without compromising safety criticality. Risk: Safety services may require non-negotiable timing overhead that conflicts with cycle time target.

Assumption: Telemetry instrumentation does not introduce measurement artifacts or overhead that distorts cycle time. Risk: Instrumentation itself may add latency or jitter that affects measured vs. actual cycle time.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2 to explicitly define the duty cycle (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator skill level), safety time budget (maximum allowable overhead per cycle phase), and telemetry measurement protocol (timestamp precision, phase detection logic). This will reduce ambiguity and enable tractable sub-problem decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. At this level, DP-2 (cycle time performance decomposition) is realized as a system-level performance contract that allocates cycle time budgets to sub-modules and coordinates their execution via shared telemetry and safety constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, safety overhead, teleoperation latency) to external monitoring and analytics systems. Used for KPI tracking and performance diagnostics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle tasking (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator mode) from site manager or autonomous planner. Defines the operational context for cycle time performance.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (constraint violations, safety overhead budget exceeded, anomalies) to Safety Manager and site operations. Enables real-time safety monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for teleoperation control, telemetry uplink, and safety alert distribution. Latency and bandwidth of Network Link directly impact cycle time performance in tele-operated mode.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level boundary for cycle time KPI realization. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution scope. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module contains powertrain, hydraulics, and motion control logic that directly impact cycle time. However, cycle time performance is not solely a Vehicle concern: teleoperation latency, safety constraints, and instrumentation are distributed across other modules. Assigning DP-2 to Vehicle alone would create high diffusion and coupling.

Teleoperation module contains network stack and operator control logic that impact tele-operated cycle time. However, cycle time is also dependent on vehicle dynamics and safety services. Assigning DP-2 to Teleoperation alone would be incomplete and create diffusion across Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services module enforces safety constraints that may impact cycle time (e.g., speed limits, acceleration limits, safety overhead). However, Safety Services is not responsible for vehicle motion optimization or teleoperation latency. Assigning DP-2 to Safety Services alone would create high diffusion and misalignment with safety criticality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-2 (cycle time performance decomposition) is best realized at the NGHE Solution level as a system-level performance contract that allocates cycle time budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules. This avoids diffusion and ensures that cycle time is treated as an emergent system property rather than a localized concern. The NGHE Solution module owns the duty cycle definition, safety time budget allocation, telemetry instrumentation schema, and integrated performance testing.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary; detailed cycle time optimization logic (e.g., powertrain control, network optimization, safety check scheduling) resides in sub-modules. NGHE Solution must define clear performance contracts and measurement protocols to coordinate sub-module behavior.

Risk: Cycle time is sensitive to external factors (network latency, site conditions, operator skill) that are not fully under NGHE Solution control. Telemetry must distinguish between system-intrinsic cycle time and external variability.

Risk: Safety criticality (high) may impose non-negotiable safety overhead that conflicts with cycle time target. NGHE Solution must manage this trade-off explicitly via safety time budget allocation and risk acceptance.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a cycle time performance contract at the NGHE Solution level that specifies: (1) duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator mode), (2) safety time budget allocation (maximum overhead per phase), (3) telemetry measurement protocol (timestamp precision, phase detection logic, external factor attribution), and (4) acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target under specified conditions). This contract will guide sub-module design and enable tractable verification.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-002) is clear and measurable, but the selected DP exhibits moderate-to-high coupling across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety domains. The DP is logically sound (decompose cycle time into four coupled sub-problems) but requires explicit coupling management via duty cycle definition, safety time budget allocation, and integrated testing. Recommend revising DP to include explicit coupling management strategy before proceeding to IM assignment.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Cycle time measurement and telemetry instrumentation are distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. NGHE Solution must define a unified telemetry schema and measurement protocol to avoid inconsistency.

Diffusion Risk 2: Safety time budget allocation requires coordination between Safety Services and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules. NGHE Solution must enforce budget constraints via explicit performance contracts.

Diffusion Risk 3: Duty cycle definition is external input (Tasking interface) but impacts all sub-modules. NGHE Solution must translate duty cycle into sub-module-specific constraints (e.g., vehicle motion envelope, teleoperation bandwidth requirements).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-2 is assigned to NGHE Solution (system-level integration module) with high containment score (95). Sub-DPs (vehicle motion optimization, teleoperation latency minimization, safety overhead budgeting, telemetry instrumentation) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively. This allocation respects module boundaries and minimizes diffusion, but requires explicit coupling management via performance contracts and integrated testing.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-002 is feasible via instrumented field trials under controlled duty cycle conditions. Telemetry interface (Telemetry) enables continuous measurement of cycle time KPI. Acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target) are measurable and objective. However, verification requires careful control of external factors (network latency, site conditions, operator skill) to isolate system-intrinsic cycle time performance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of DP-2 to NGHE Solution is clear: NGHE Solution owns the cycle time performance contract and coordinates sub-module execution. Sub-DPs are allocated to Vehicle (motion optimization), Teleoperation (latency minimization), Safety Services (overhead budgeting), and NGHE Solution (telemetry instrumentation). Allocation is traceable and supports verification.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1 (DP): Refine DP-2 to explicitly define coupling management strategy, including duty cycle definition, safety time budget allocation, telemetry measurement protocol, and integrated testing approach.

Revision 2 (IM): Define a cycle time performance contract at the NGHE Solution level that specifies duty cycle, safety time budget, telemetry schema, and acceptance criteria. This contract will guide sub-module design and enable tractable verification.

Revision 3 (FR): Clarify the relationship between CR-NGHE-002 (cycle time KPI) and safety criticality (high). Explicitly state that safety constraints are non-negotiable and may impose overhead that erodes cycle time. Define acceptable trade-offs.

Revision 4 (Verification): Define a verification protocol that distinguishes between system-intrinsic cycle time performance and external variability (network latency, site conditions, operator skill). This will enable fair assessment of NGHE Solution performance.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI requirement that cannot be satisfied by a single module or simple DP. The refined FR is clear and measurable, but the selected DP exhibits moderate-to-high coupling across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety domains. Proceeding directly to sub-level decomposition (ZIGZAG_DOWN) is appropriate to manage coupling and allocate cycle time budgets to sub-modules. However, before decomposing further, recommend revising DP-2 to include explicit coupling management strategy (duty cycle definition, safety time budget allocation, telemetry measurement protocol) to ensure sub-level DPs are well-constrained and traceable.
decision.justification_detail: The gate review identified that DP-2 is logically sound but requires explicit coupling management. ZIGZAG_DOWN allows us to decompose cycle time into four coupled sub-problems (vehicle motion, teleoperation latency, safety overhead, telemetry instrumentation) and allocate them to sub-modules. This will enable tractable design and verification. However, the coupling management strategy must be defined at the current level (NGHE Solution) before sub-level decomposition proceeds.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions within motion envelope constraints to support cycle time target.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control responsiveness (latency, bandwidth) sufficient to achieve cycle time target when tele-operated.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding a defined safety-overhead time budget that erodes cycle time.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration) to enable KPI tracking.

revise.why: DP-2 is logically sound but lacks explicit coupling management strategy. Recommend revising DP-2 to include: (1) duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator mode), (2) safety time budget allocation (maximum overhead per phase), (3) telemetry measurement protocol (timestamp precision, phase detection logic), and (4) integrated testing approach. This will reduce ambiguity and enable tractable sub-problem decomposition.

assumptions: Assumption 1: CT_target is achievable within the vehicle's physical motion envelope and safety constraints. Requires validation against vehicle dynamics simulation and safety analysis.
Assumption 2: Teleoperation latency can be reduced to acceptable levels via network optimization. Requires assessment of physical network constraints (e.g., satellite uplink, 4G coverage).
Assumption 3: Safety overhead can be budgeted and allocated without compromising safety criticality. Requires safety analysis and risk acceptance.
Assumption 4: Telemetry instrumentation does not introduce measurement artifacts or overhead that distorts cycle time. Requires instrumentation design review and validation.
Assumption 5: Duty cycle definition is stable and well-understood by stakeholders. Requires stakeholder alignment on load mass, travel distance, terrain type, and operator mode.
open_risks: Risk 1 (High): CT_target may be unrealistic given vehicle dynamics and safety constraints. Mitigation: Validate CT_target against vehicle dynamics simulation and safety analysis early in design.

Risk 2 (High): Safety overhead may exceed allocated time budget, eroding cycle time performance. Mitigation: Conduct safety analysis to quantify overhead; define acceptable trade-offs between safety and cycle time.

Risk 3 (Medium): Teleoperation latency may be dominated by physical network constraints (e.g., satellite uplink) rather than system design. Mitigation: Assess network constraints early; consider hybrid autonomous/tele-operated modes to mitigate latency impact.

Risk 4 (Medium): Cycle time variability may be high due to external factors (site conditions, operator skill, network jitter). Mitigation: Define duty cycle carefully; use p95 percentile (not mean) as acceptance criterion to account for variability.

Risk 5 (Medium): Telemetry instrumentation may introduce latency or jitter that affects measured vs. actual cycle time. Mitigation: Design instrumentation with minimal overhead; validate measurement accuracy via independent timing reference.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Vehicle dynamics simulation showing achievability of CT_target under specified duty cycle and safety constraints.
Evidence 2: Network latency analysis showing feasibility of teleoperation latency target under expected site conditions.
Evidence 3: Safety analysis quantifying safety overhead per cycle phase and demonstrating compatibility with cycle time budget.
Evidence 4: Telemetry instrumentation design review and validation showing measurement accuracy and minimal overhead.
Evidence 5: Stakeholder alignment on duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator mode) and acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target).
Evidence 6: Integrated performance testing under controlled conditions demonstrating p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target.
